javax.media.mscontrol.mediagroup.signals
Interface SignalGenerator

All Superinterfaces:
MediaEventNotifier<SignalGeneratorEvent>, Resource<ResourceContainer>
public interface SignalGenerator
extends Resource<ResourceContainer>, MediaEventNotifier<SignalGeneratorEvent>

Defines methods for sending signals out to the network.

Field Summary
static Parameter INTER_SIGNAL_GAP
          Pause duration between each DTMF, as a positive Integer, in milliseconds
static Parameter SIGNAL_AMPLITUDE
          DTMF modulation level, in dB.
static Parameter SIGNAL_LENGTH
          DTMF duration, as a positive Integer, in milliseconds

          Transmit a series of signals out the Terminal to the network.

 void stop()

Field Detail

INTER_SIGNAL_GAP

static final Parameter INTER_SIGNAL_GAP
Pause duration between each DTMF, as a positive Integer, in milliseconds

SIGNAL_LENGTH

static final Parameter SIGNAL_LENGTH
DTMF duration, as a positive Integer, in milliseconds

SIGNAL_AMPLITUDE

static final Parameter SIGNAL_AMPLITUDE
DTMF modulation level, in dB.
Value must be an Integer, in the range [0...-9].
Note: On a T1 Network, dropping the level may be useful to prevent the DTMF from activating AGC.

Method Detail

emitSignals

void emitSignals(Value[] signals,
                 RTC[] rtc,
                 Parameters optargs)
                 throws MsControlException
Transmit a series of signals out the Terminal to the network.

Each signal is defined by a Value.

Parameters:
signals - an array of Value's that each defines a signal.
rtc - an array of RTC objects that controls this transaction. The RTCs are only active once emitSignals() has been invoked and up to the completion of emitSignals() operation, independently of which resource is referenced in the RTC's Trigger and Action.
optargs - a Parameters map of optional arguments
Throws:
java.lang.IllegalStateException - if the hosting container has been released
MsControlException
See Also:
MediaEventListener

emitSignals

void emitSignals(java.lang.String signals,
                 RTC[] rtc,
                 Parameters optargs)
                 throws MsControlException
Transmit a series of signals out the Terminal to the network.

Each signal is defined by a char in the String.

Throws:
MsControlException
See Also:
emitSignals(Value[], RTC[], Parameters)

stop

void stop()
Stops a emitSignal() operation that is running, it will terminate and send SignalGeneratorEvent.EMIT_SIGNALS_COMPLETED. The completion event may usually contain the qualifier ResourceEvent.STOPPED if the operation has actually been stopped upon effect of the call, however the application must not assume this is always the case. The application shall make no assumption about what Signals may have already been sent out before the stop is actually processed. If no operation is running, or has already been stopped but the event not received by the application yet, the call is void.
